prompt:
Add version/commit display to the app UI. The user wants to see what version/commit/branch is deployed, especially useful across staging/preview/prod. ## Approach ### 1. Generate version.json at build time (flake.nix) In `flake.nix`, the `packages.default` derivation (line 51) should generate a `version.json` file during `installPhase`. Nix flakes expose `self.rev` (full SHA) and `self.shortRev` (7-char) when built from a clean git tree, and `self.dirtyRev`/`self.dirtyShortRev` for dirty trees. Change the derivation to accept the rev. Use nix string interpolation of `self.shortRev or "dev"` to embed the commit hash into a version.json written in installPhase. Note: `self.shortRev` is only available when the tree is clean. Use `self.shortRev or "dev"` for dirty/local builds. Also make sure timer.js and manifest.json are copied in installPhase (they should already be there from a recent fix). ### 2. Fetch and display version in the app (app.js + index.html + style.css) In `app.js`: - Add an async function `loadVersion()` that fetches `version.json` (with a try/catch for 404 in dev mode) - Display the commit in the settings modal via `document.getElementById("version-info")` - Format as: `v1.0.0 · abc1234` - Call `loadVersion()` during `init()` In `index.html`, add a version display element at the bottom of the settings modal content (just before the closing `` of `.settings-content`, around line 94): ```html
``` In `style.css`, add minimal styling for `.version-info`: small font (0.7rem), muted color (var(--text-secondary)), centered, with a top border separator, opacity 0.6. ### Key files: - `flake.nix` — derivation installPhase (line 51-61) - `app.js` — add loadVersion function, call in init() - `index.html` — add version-info div in settings modal (before line 94) - `style.css` — add .version-info styles ### Constraints: - Keep it subtle — small muted text at the bottom of the settings modal - Gracefully handle missing version.json (dev mode / local serve) - Do not break existing e2e tests - Run the e2e tests before creating the PR: `npx playwright test` - The flake.nix change is important — the nix expression must be valid. The interpolation of `self.shortRev` happens at the flake level via nix string interpolation, not bash.
